items.title: FINAL: Knicks 86, Pistons 75. They fall to 1-2 overall. Another strong performance from Chaz Lanier (24 points, 8-12 from 3). Ebuka Okorie finished with 14 points, 6 rebounds and 4 assists but shot 5-18 overall. He's been adjusting to the physicality.

items.title: Pistons beat Cavs 103-94. One of the better games this summer, overall. Good stuff from Cavs' rookie Meleek Thomas, some notable defensive flashes from Ebuka Okorie for Detroit (seven steals!), and an Emergent Random Guy in the Pistons' Brice Williams.
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Pistons beat Cavs 103-94. One of the better games this summer, overall. Good stuff from Cavs' rookie Meleek Thomas, some notable defensive flashes from Ebuka Okorie for Detroit (seven steals!), and an Emergent Random Guy in the Pistons' Brice Williams.

items.title: Going through Trajan's quotes, one thing that sticks out is how much he emphasizes Okorie's ability to generate paint touches in halfcourt. The offense was very transition-reliant last year, which hurt them in the playoffs. Okorie addresses that.
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Going through Trajan's quotes, one thing that sticks out is how much he emphasizes Okorie's ability to generate paint touches in halfcourt. The offense was very transition-reliant last year, which hurt them in the playoffs. Okorie addresses that.

items.title: Langdon on if drafting Okorie will impact their offseason plans: "We’ll continue to look to get better. He helps us moving forward but we will continue to be aggressive and look for ways to get better."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Langdon on if drafting Okorie will impact their offseason plans: "We’ll continue to look to get better. He helps us moving forward but we will continue to be aggressive and look for ways to get better."

items.title: Langdon on how Okorie can bring a different dynamic to the backcourt: "Anybody that can push pace the way he can and come in with the second unit … will make the game easier for a lot of people. And he can pick up full court and press opposing ballhandlers."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Langdon on how Okorie can bring a different dynamic to the backcourt: "Anybody that can push pace the way he can and come in with the second unit … will make the game easier for a lot of people. And he can pick up full court and press opposing ballhandlers."

items.title: Get To Know Detroit Pistons No. 17 Overall Draft Pick Ebuka Okorie
items.author: Sports Illustrated
items.body_excerpt: Get To Know Detroit Pistons No. 17 Overall Draft Pick Ebuka Okorie Sports Illustrated

items.title: Langdon's repeated he has no doubt Okorie will reach his potential. "He plays bigger than he is, listed at 6-2 but he’s got a 6-8 wingspan … his body can develop and get stronger. He’s going to reach his potential and his ceiling, because that’s the type of young man he is."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Langdon's repeated he has no doubt Okorie will reach his potential. "He plays bigger than he is, listed at 6-2 but he’s got a 6-8 wingspan … his body can develop and get stronger. He’s going to reach his potential and his ceiling, because that’s the type of young man he is."

items.title: Langdon said Okorie was someone they liked through the second half of the season and targeted. "He can make us faster and be another guy on the floor who can handle the ball."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Langdon said Okorie was someone they liked through the second half of the season and targeted. "He can make us faster and be another guy on the floor who can handle the ball."

items.title: Trajan Langdon leads off praising Ebuka Okorie’s speed, ability to get downhill with his ballhandling and overall character
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Trajan Langdon leads off praising Ebuka Okorie’s speed, ability to get downhill with his ballhandling and overall character

items.title: Okorie: "A lot of my confidence and the comfortability comes from the reps. I’m repping out pick and rolls, knowing what defenders I’m reading and trying to make the best play possible, both for myself and my teammates."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Okorie: "A lot of my confidence and the comfortability comes from the reps. I’m repping out pick and rolls, knowing what defenders I’m reading and trying to make the best play possible, both for myself and my teammates."

items.title: Okorie on his outside shooting: "I'm going to stay in the gym, continue to work on my game. I’m not worried about shooting at all."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Okorie on his outside shooting: "I'm going to stay in the gym, continue to work on my game. I’m not worried about shooting at all."

items.title: Okorie on his role: "I feel like alongside Cade I’ll be another playmaker on the floor, another shot creator able to create for myself and others, and on the defensive end just bringing intensity, using my physicality and getting in passing lanes."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Okorie on his role: "I feel like alongside Cade I’ll be another playmaker on the floor, another shot creator able to create for myself and others, and on the defensive end just bringing intensity, using my physicality and getting in passing lanes."

items.title: Okorie: "When I came for the workout I was greeted with a lot of hospitality. Everyone was very welcoming. The workout was very high level, high-intensity. Sitting down and eating with Trajan Langdon and the front office, I got a really good feel."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Okorie: "When I came for the workout I was greeted with a lot of hospitality. Everyone was very welcoming. The workout was very high level, high-intensity. Sitting down and eating with Trajan Langdon and the front office, I got a really good feel."
